meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Tokyo theme park worker dies after being trapped inside ride mechanism during routine maintenance
items.summary: An employee conducting routine maintenance at Tokyo Dome City Attractions reportedly died after becoming trapped in the "Flying Balloon" ride mechanism.

items.title: Pope Leo urges Africans to stay and 'serve your country' instead of migrating as displacement climbs
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV urged African youth to stay and improve their own countries rather than migrate while speaking to university students in Cameroon's capital.

items.title: Israel unveils game-changing artillery against Iran-backed Hezbollah amid fragile ceasefire
items.summary: Israel deploys its new self-propelled Ro'em howitzer against Hezbollah targets as ceasefire talks between Israel and Lebanon slated to continue in Washington later this week.

items.title: Mexico temple gunman ranted about pyramid's gruesome history to hostages: ‘Don’t move, or I’ll sacrifice you’
items.summary: Video shows the Teotihuacan pyramids gunman reportedly bragging about sacrificing victims before killing a Canadian tourist and wounding others.

items.title: UK’s elite soldier unit faces exodus over ‘lawfare’ fears — warning sign for US military?
items.summary: Britain's elite SAS faces a growing exodus as soldiers resign over fears of legal scrutiny for actions in Afghanistan and other conflicts, report says.

items.title: Mexican president in the dark about drug operation after which US, Mexican officials were killed
items.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum seeks explanations regarding what U.S. and Mexican officials were doing when deadly accident occurred.

items.title: Mexico pyramid shooter who took hostages and killed 1 is identified
items.summary: Officials identified the gunman who fatally shot a Canadian tourist and wounded others atop a historic pyramid at Teotihuacan in Mexico on Monday.

items.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard sidelines president as military grip expands
items.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps effectively has assumed control over key state functions and blocked presidential appointments, according to an Iran International report.

items.title: Deadly shooting at historic tourist site leaves one dead, several injured as motive unclear
items.summary: A shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacan pyramids left a Canadian woman dead and multiple tourists injured, according to Mexican officials and security reports.

items.title: Who is Ahmad Vahidi? Iran’s new IRGC chief tied to global attacks and ‘Death to America’ ideology
items.summary: Shadowy IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi is emerging as one of Iran's most powerful figures as Trump's ceasefire deadline approaches, analysts warn.

items.title: Carnival Cruise passenger, 67, found dead while snorkeling off the coast of Australia during excursion
items.summary: A Carnival Splendor passenger reportedly drowned while snorkeling near Moreton Island, Australia, during a four-night roundtrip cruise from Sydney.

items.title: Massive 7.7-magnitude earthquake hits off Japanese coast
items.summary: A strong earthquake erupted off the coast of the nation of Japan, the Associated Press reported, noting the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center indicated the tsunami threat "has now passed."

items.title: Distress call captures tanker under fire, Iran shuts Hormuz trapping thousands of sailors
items.summary: Hundreds of oil tankers are stranded at the Strait of Hormuz after Iran shut the chokepoint, with crews reporting gunfire and traumatic experiences.

items.title: Two US Embassy personnel killed in 'accident' in Mexico, ambassador says
items.summary: Two U.S. Embassy personnel were killed in an "accident" in Mexico, according to U.S. Ambassador to Mexico Ronald Johnson. Details remain unclear.

items.title: Hezbollah ‘human shield’ strategy behind Lebanon ambush, bomb detonation - Macron drawn in
items.summary: A French soldier was killed in a southern Lebanon ambush allegedly carried out by Hezbollah, straining the 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreement.

items.title: Slain Iranian nuclear scientists raises alarm over uranium, expertise reaching black market
items.summary: The killing of Iranian nuclear scientists in U.S.-Israeli strikes raises fears over nuclear proliferation as experts warn of weakened material controls.

items.title: UK chief rabbi says Jews targeted by ‘sustained campaign of violence and intimidation' after string of attacks
items.summary: Police probe suspected Iranian proxy group allegedly behind a wave of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in London, raising alarm across Europe.

items.title: Trump criticizes Spain amid Iran, NATO rift as PM Sanchez faces questions over political motives
items.summary: Spain's PM Pedro Sanchez is leveraging his stances on Trump, Iran, and Israel to coalesce the global left amid mounting political scandals involving his wife and brother.

items.title: Moscow-born gunman dead after Kyiv shooting rampage leaves at least 6 dead, 14 wounded: Zelenskyy
items.summary: Kyiv supermarket shooting leaves at least 6 dead, several wounded after a Russian gunman took hostages and opened fire, according to Ukrainian officials.

items.title: Pope Leo says remarks about world being 'ravaged by a ​handful of tyrants' were not aimed at Trump: report
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV said his remarks about the world being ravaged by tyrants were not directed at President Donald Trump amid their ongoing public exchange of words.

items.title: Trump’s favorite field marshal: Who is Pakistan’s powerful army chief Asim Munir with deep intel ties
items.summary: How Pakistan's military chief Asim Munir became one of the few foreign figures trusted by both Trump and Iran's security establishment today.

items.title: Alleged Irish cartel boss arrested in covert operation on organized crime charges after years-long manhunt
items.summary: International fugitive Daniel Kinahan, alleged leader of Ireland's Kinahan cartel, was arrested in Dubai this week on organized crime charges.

items.title: Escaped wolf Neukgu returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search involving thermal imaging drones
items.summary: Neukgu, a 2-year-old wolf, escaped a South Korean zoo nine days ago and was safely captured after an extensive search involving drones and more.

items.title: Bride’s sister-in-law douses her in black paint moments before ceremony in horrifying ‘revenge’ attack
items.summary: Bride Gemma Monk says a black paint attack by her sister-in-law just before her wedding has left her unable to work and battling depression.

items.title: Australia's most decorated veteran walks free on bail on war crimes charges related to Afghan deaths
items.summary: Australia's most decorated living veteran Ben Roberts-Smith was released on bail after being charged with war crime murder over killings in Afghanistan.
